commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds PTY-based TUI e2e tests, a dedicated TUI PTY Vitest config and workflow, package watch scripts, a watch helper, and related testing docs.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR-head routing defect. Source inspection shows the new PTY config owns e2e files while current main's full-suite shard and leaf-shard expectations only list the normal TUI config.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can wire the new PTY config into the full-suite shard/routing expectations and run the focused checks.

target.title: Add TUI PTY integration coverage
target.body:
Summary
- Add PTY-based TUI integration tests covering real terminal input/render behavior against a deterministic fake TuiBackend fixture.
- Add a local-backend PTY smoke test that runs tui --local with an isolated config and mocks only the external OpenAI-compatible model endpoint.
- Add a focused TUI PTY GitHub Actions workflow plus a watch command for recording the actual test run.

Verification
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui-pty-harness.test.ts src/tui/tui-pty-local.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/package-scripts.test.ts
- pnpm tui:pty:test:watch --no-alt-screen
- git diff --check

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: TUI PTY tests now exercise startup, typed prompt submission, rendered responses, slash command routing, busy guards, model patching, fast-mode status, reset, and clean exit.
Real environment tested: Local macOS worktree using node-pty pseudo-terminals; CI workflow targets Ubuntu 24.04 with Node 24.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui-pty-harness.test.ts src/tui/tui-pty-local.test.ts
Evidence after fix: 2 test files passed, 11 tests passed.
Observed result after fix: Fake-backend PTY coverage and local-backend PTY smoke coverage both pass; watch mode can mirror the test TUI without mixing Vitest reporter output into the alternate-screen view.
What was not tested: Live provider calls, real external model APIs, full release CI, and broad cross-OS TUI behavior.
target.author: romneyda
